mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with falling housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening consumer demand and construction activity, signaling reduced near-term corporate revenue growth and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 0.5% signals strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, indicating robust economic activity that supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO > 0.5% increase are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals accelerating labor market weakness that reduces household income and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100k and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint deterioration in PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) above 3% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2%) signals monetary tightening to combat inflation, reducing equity valuations through higher discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when both inflation and rates are elevated, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.2% signals strengthening residential construction demand and forward-looking consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as construction and related sectors benefit.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 1.2% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following sustained HOUST increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly at levels below 1.5% annualized signals economic stagnation that constrains corporate revenue growth and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors discount slower profit expansion.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of quarters with GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
